News scan: only stale items found (bemotrizinol = Jun 9; a search-surfaced "Jul 10 recall" was a July 2025 article on its visible dateline) — news empty today.

her_wins.summary: Glass-skin routine — superviral on BOTH platforms Wed: TT 1.2M in 2.7d (51×, 3% breakout, GOLD follower magnet, top-1% engagement) + IG 1.2M (12.7×, 3%, silver ♥). This one is generating FOLLOWERS, not just views.
her_wins.open_lane: Extend, don't pivot: part 2 answering the comment questions (pregnancy-safe swaps, exfoliation cadence) in the same 7–8s format; the quiz variant can wait.

stitch_shortlist.author: @rob.lea1
stitch_shortlist.claim: Diaper cream + Zinka + Bag Balm = 11-hour open-ocean sun protection

pitch.the_claim: Marathon swimmer survives 11+ hours crossing the Tsugaru Strait slathered in diaper rash cream, Zinka and Bag Balm — presented as sun protection that works.
pitch.why_it_stitches: 15.9M views at ~3.8M/day pace, 4.1K comments; half-right is the best trigger class (zinc oxide IS a UV filter); punches up at an elite athlete, not down; the derm in-group is circling (drdrayzday already responded — yours needs a NEW angle).
pitch.your_fit: You are already pro-zinc-oxide on camera: "look for a mineral sunscreen with zinc oxide or titanium dioxide" (rosacea video, 1.5M) — and you recommend zinc-oxide diaper cream at night as barrier repair (2.4M video) and for perioral dermatitis (875K YT). New angle vs "it's not sunscreen": when the diaper-cream trick actually works, when it fails, and the 2-hour reapply rule.
angles: "You want me to WHAT?" — Bag Balm-on-face verdict from a Mohs surgeon
half-right teach: zinc oxide is a real UV filter — thickness, coverage and water time are the variables
empathy-first: 11-hour outdoor athletes deserve a real protocol — here's mine

stitch_shortlist.author: @ambie.gail
stitch_shortlist.claim: "I swear I wear sunscreen — I still burn every time"

pitch.the_claim: Serial sunburner insists she really does wear sunscreen and still ends up burnt (3.4M views of relatable burn results).
pitch.why_it_stitches: Huge relatable audience; the half-right gap (wearing ≠ enough/reapplied) is a pure teaching lane; empathy-first keeps it punching sideways, never down.
pitch.your_fit: Your reapply doctrine is verbatim on camera: "always reapply every 2 hours if you can, but don't let perfect be the enemy of good" (469K) + reapply-while-exercising video (347K).
angles: empathy-first: you're not lying — here are the 4 ways sunscreen "fails" you
burn audit: quantity, timing, reapplication, UV index — which one is it?

stitch_shortlist.author: @danie1lek
stitch_shortlist.claim: This skin-prep + SPF routine = seamless makeup base AND real protection

pitch.the_claim: Skin-prep-plus-SPF tutorial promising a seamless makeup base while staying protected (1.4M views).
pitch.why_it_stitches: 5,961 comments on 1.4M views — wildly disproportionate; the SPF-under-makeup adequacy question is exactly the argument raging in those comments.
pitch.your_fit: You already teach tinted mineral sunscreen as the makeup-adjacent option ("the tinted part is great if you have rosacea… blocks visible light"). The application-amount and reapply-over-makeup specifics are the gap to fill.
angles: does makeup-grade SPF application actually protect? the quantity rule
reapplying over makeup without wrecking it — needs your on-camera take

stitch_shortlist.author: @skincarewithyuri
stitch_shortlist.claim: Daily olive-oil + lemon shots and collagen-rich food = "filter" skin

pitch.the_claim: "Filter routine": olive oil & lemon shot every day, eat collagen-rich foods, and your skin looks filtered (618K in 1.9d, gold ♥ breakout — plus a 316K gelatin-recipe follow-up at 631K/day pace).
pitch.why_it_stitches: Rising fast on reach AND engagement; half-right claims from a skinfluencer punch sideways; collagen questions flood every derm comment section.
pitch.your_fit: You said on camera you were "actually surprised" the systematic reviews show collagen supplements DO help skin appearance — especially fish and porcine collagen — after explaining digestion breaks it down ("like taking a house down to its bricks and rebuilding"). No such data for olive-oil-lemon shots.
angles: "I was surprised by the evidence" — what holds up (collagen: sort of; shots: no)
the house-into-bricks digestion metaphor, on camera
swap-don't-quit: keep the collagen-rich food, skip the lemon-oil shot

stitch_shortlist.author: @robertwblove
stitch_shortlist.claim: Avoid these sunscreens — they harm your hormones

pitch.the_claim: List-style warning that specific sunscreens "harm your hormones," aimed at parents (530K in 3d, 865 comments).
pitch.why_it_stitches: The hormone-fear wave converts viewers into sunscreen-skippers — dangerous trigger; still rising; wellness creator = punching sideways/up.
pitch.your_fit: You own the credible middle ON CAMERA: you avoid certain chemical filters for children, pregnancy, and hormone-sensitive cancers because "some of these chemicals have been shown to be known hormone disruptors" (Mar 2023) — while recommending chemical sunscreens for darker skin (Aug 2025 stitch) and calling newly-approved bemotrizinol safe, with no estrogenic activity, 7.6d ago (308K IG).
angles: the honest hormone answer: which filters, which people, what the data says
risk math from a Mohs surgeon: hormone fear vs the skin cancer I remove daily
bemotrizinol peg: the brand-new filter with no estrogenic activity

stitch_shortlist.author: @heyitsmans
stitch_shortlist.claim: "I'd rather get cancer the natural way than the chemical way"

pitch.the_claim: Creator reacts, shaken, to the viral line "I'd rather get cancer the natural way than the chemical way" (378K views, 2.7K comments).
pitch.why_it_stitches: 2,715 comments on 378K views — the quote is doing the work; stitching the QUOTE punches up at the rhetoric, and a Mohs surgeon is the single most credible voice against it.
pitch.your_fit: "There's no such thing as a 'healthy tan' — it's literally DNA damage" (your Jul 2025 caption, verbatim) + what you cut out of faces every week.
angles: Mohs surgeon hears "natural cancer" — here's what natural looks like in clinic
empathy-first: where the chemical fear comes from, then the actual risk ledger
